title: Examine preventing CPD hire of officers from ICE / HSI / CBP hired on or after Jan 20, 2025
summary: The Council asked the City Manager to examine whether and how Cambridge can prevent the Police Department from hiring any officer hired by ICE, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol on or after January 20, 2025, and report back. Adopted as amended unanimously.
body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to direct the relevant City staff and departments to examine if and how the City may prevent the hiring by the Cambridge Police Department of any sworn officer who was hired on or after January 20, 2025 by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol, and report back to the City Council in a timely manner. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]

title: Protecting Academic Freedom and Condemning Attacks on Higher Education
summary: A policy order titled "Protecting Academic Freedom and Condemning Attacks on Higher Education" was adopted unanimously. Cambridge is home to several major universities affected by federal higher-education policy.
body: Protecting Academic Freedom and Condemning Attacks on Higher Education. COUNCILLOR NOLAN, MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR AL-ZUBI, VICE MAYOR AZEEM. RESULT: Adopted [9-0-0]

rationale.doc.sponsor:azeem: VICE MAYOR AZEEM listed as sponsor
rationale.work.student:undergrad: academic freedom protections affect undergraduate students
rationale.work.student:grad: academic freedom protections affect graduate students

title: Examine permitting third-party guarantors / co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants
summary: The Council asked the Community Development Department to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants, and recommend whether such a policy could responsibly expand access. Adopted 8-0-1, with Councillor Zusy voting Present.
body: That the City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department (CDD) to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified thirdparty guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units and provide recommendations as to whether such a policy could responsibly expand access to these units. RESULT: Adopted [8-0-1-0]

rationale.doc.sponsor:azeem: VICE MAYOR AZEEM listed as sponsor
rationale.housing.tenure:rent: Inclusionary Housing units are typically rental

title: Draft ordinance restricting use of City property and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement
summary: The Council asked the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement, and to evaluate whether the protections should be added to the Welcoming Community Ordinance. Adopted as amended unanimously.
body: That the City Manager is hereby requested to direct the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for the purposes of federal civil immigration enforcement, and that the Law Department evaluate whether these protections should be adopted as an amendment to the Welcoming Community Ordinance. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]

rationale.doc.sponsor:zusy: COUNCILLOR ZUSY added as sponsor in Final Actions
rationale.doc.sponsor:al_zubi: COUNCILLOR AL-ZUBI added as sponsor in Final Actions

title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75, $25 subsidy for low-income; remove senior exemption; cap at 2 cars/household
summary: A policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, a subsidized $25 fee for residents in affordable housing or SNAP, removal of the existing senior exemption, and a cap of 2 permits per household (down from 4). Charter Right exercised by Councillor Simmons; no final action at this meeting.
body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. RESULT: Charter Right

title: Plan to expand free early child care offerings, including means-tested models and outside funding
summary: The Council asked the City Manager to work with the Council and relevant departments on expanding free early child care offerings, including means-tested programs and exploration of state and foundation funding. Charter Right exercised by Councillor Simmons; no final action at this meeting.
body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the City Council and relevant City departments on the process by which Cambridge can expand free early child care offerings, including models for means-tested programming, and exploration of non-City funding sources, from the state or foundations. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. RESULT: Charter Right

title: Plan with MIT and CASPAR to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street shelter, with low-threshold support
summary: The Council asked the City Manager to begin discussions with MIT, CASPAR, the Human Services and Veterans Committee co-chairs, relevant departments, and shelter residents to develop a plan to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street, providing additional low-threshold shelter, housing, and daytime services and accommodating residents impacted by construction. Adopted unanimously.
body: That the City Manager is requested to begin discussions with MIT, CASPAR, the co-chairs of the Human Services and Veterans Committee, all relevant city departments and the shelter residents to develop a plan to renovate and expand 240 Albany Street in order to provide additional low threshold shelter, housing and day time services, while accommodating shelter residents impacted by the construction closure with housing and support, including low threshold options. RESULT: Adopted [9-0-0]

title: Policy Order Supporting Union Recognition at Lamplighter Brewing Co.
summary: A late-filed policy order supporting union recognition at Lamplighter Brewing Co., with appreciation for the owners' community contributions. Adopted as amended unanimously.
body: Policy Order Supporting Union Recognition at Lamplighter Brewing Co., with Appreciation for the Owners' Community Contributions. COUNCILLOR MCGOVERN, MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR SIMMONS, COUNCILLOR AL-ZUBI, COUNCILLOR SOBRINHO WHEELER, COUNCILLOR NOLAN. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]
